$schema_note: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) index — legend, ID scheme, phase map, and dependency/critical-path notes for the Legacy IT Student Portal. Generated from definition_data/** and docs/index.html (BSIT interactive prototype). This is a TEMPLATE: structural fields (id, title, description, deliverables, acceptance_criteria, depends_on, definition_refs) are fully populated from the definitions; execution fields (estimate, priority, assignee, dates, status) are intentionally null/placeholder — fill them in using the answers you provide in ../../SystemDefinition5.txt.
how_to_use: Read every task's definition_refs to see exactly which definition_data section it implements.
Fill execution fields per task: estimate {value, unit, confidence}, priority, assignee, status, start_date, due_date, risk, notes.
Answer SystemDefinition5.txt first — its answers (team size, timeline, hosting, plugin choices) determine most of the placeholder values below.
Do not fold or split tasks unless the underlying definition_data changes — keep this file's IDs stable so external trackers (Jira/GitHub Projects/Notion) can reference them permanently.

When a phase begins, promote its file's tasks into whatever live project-management tool the team chooses (see 12-resourcing-timeline-template.json); this file remains the source-of-truth WBS, the live tool is the day-to-day view.

category_abbr.DB: database (Prisma schema, migrations, seed data, indexes)
category_abbr.SEC: security (auth, crypto, OWASP hardening, audit wiring)
category_abbr.QA: testing (unit, integration, component, E2E, load, accessibility)

id_scheme.example: P1.QR.BE03 = Phase 1, QR Attendance work package, 3rd backend task

task_object_shape.id: string — stable WBS id, see id_scheme
task_object_shape.title: string
task_object_shape.module: module-registry.json key this task belongs to
task_object_shape.definition_refs: array of definition_data/docs.html pointers this task implements
task_object_shape.deliverables: array of concrete outputs (files, endpoints, pages, migrations)
task_object_shape.acceptance_criteria: array — how you know the task is actually done
task_object_shape.depends_on: array of WBS ids that must be done first

phase_map.name: Announcements, Extended Student Profile, Digital ID

critical_path_notes: P0 (monorepo, Postgres, CI, base infra) blocks everything — nothing in P1 can start until packages/shared and the Prisma schema exist.
P1.AUTH and P1.OSAS block almost every other P1 work package: students/faculty identity, session auth, and route guards all depend on them.
P1.CATALOG (subjects/sections/offerings/schedules/enrollments) must exist before P1.QR can generate real sessions.

P1.QR (dynamic QR engine + scan validation chain) is the highest-risk, highest-priority work package — it is the system's primary module and the reason the project exists. It blocks P1.MONITORING, P1.CORRECTIONS, and most of P1.REPORTS/P1.DASHBOARD.

P1.CROSSCUTTING (QA + security hardening) should run continuously alongside every work package, not as a phase-end activity — testing_definition.json requires tests in the same PR as new business rules.

P1.DEVOPS (staging/production deploy, backups, monitoring) is the gate before go-live; testing-definition.json's E2E journeys and Department Admin staging sign-off (deployment-definition.json#environments.staging.gate) must pass first.

P2/P3/P4 files are intentionally shallow — module-registry.json only defines them at summary level; expand each into a full work-package breakdown (same depth as P1) when that phase is actually scheduled.
